rationale: Both candidates correctly bisect the failure to PR #4834's intentional level-2 wake guard, matching yenuo26's comment, but both overclaim a settled resolution ('already fixed on main', 'close as resolved') that the actual thread never confirms — the thread ends with Flink-ddd asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI sleep-mode run, i.e. still unverified, and neither candidate engages with that pending action item. Y is more thoroughly grounded, citing the sleep()-side flag-setting code (946-947), the wake_up() guard (953-959), and a related generate()-time guard (319-324), plus offers concrete runtime guidance distinguishing level-1 vs level-2 use — giving a more complete, end-to-end answer than X's slightly thinner writeup. Neither is clearly 'wrong' since the underlying diagnosis is plausible, but both substitute confident closure for the genuine open-endedness the thread shows.
